result:
I would make every human mind incapable of sustaining hatred without feeling the pain it inflicts on others—an immediate, visceral feedback loop that forces empathy. Not a removal of anger or disagreement, but a collapse of the illusion that cruelty can be externalized. The moment you intend harm, you experience the exact emotional and physical weight of what you're about to do, as if it's happening to you. No shield of ideology, no distance of dehumanization. Just the bare, reflexive knowledge: *this is what it feels like*.  

The world wouldn't become gentle overnight—people would still clash, still struggle, still fail. But the machinery of systematic harm—war, genocide, slavery, ecocide—would seize up when its operators couldn't endure the torque of their own actions. A single gear refusing to turn.
